LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION HELD ON MAR-30-2015, IN DEPT. 302. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE THERE EXISTS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE HEAD RESTRAINT WAS DEFECTIVE BECAUSE IT LACKED A LOCKING SYSTEM. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF MALICE AND PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO A CONTINUANCE PER CCP 437C(H). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302 (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-30-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar May-13-2015 Continued To Jun-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-30-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SCOTT RIGHTHAND, Tel No. (415) 544-0115 appearing for Plaintiffs Leland Hague and Tiffany Hague;
Atty. KRISTINE W. HANSON of King & Spalding LLP, Tel No. (415) 318-1213 appearing for Defendants Winkel Motors Inc., General Motors LLC, and Geo. Growney Motors, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS WINKEL MOTORS INC., GENERAL MOTORS LLC, AND GEO. GROWNEY MOTORS, INC.'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, the motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The court denies summary adjudication as to plaintiff's causes of action because there exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the head restraint was defective because it lacked a locking system. Plaintiff's declaration and the declaration of Mr. Forrest are admissible. Plaintiff is describing what he personally witnessed when the head restraint was allegedly pushed down. Mr. Forrest opines that the head restraint was defective because it did not have a locking device. The court grants summary adjudication on the claim for punitive damages. There is no evidence of malice and plaintiff is not entitled to a continuance per CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff has not been diligently conducting discovery and counsel's declaration does highlight what facts counsel hopes to discover. See Cooksey v. Alexakis (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 246, 257. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
